title: Best Mattress for Side Sleepers (2026): How We Rank From Review Intelligence
description: A practical guide to reading SleepChoice pressure and support scores when you sleep on your side—plus which registry axes matter most before you buy.

sections.heading: Why side sleepers need different scores
paragraphs: Side sleeping concentrates force on shoulders and hips. In our registry, the Pressure Relief index proxies how often aggregated owner reviews mention hip/shoulder discomfort, while Support reflects spinal alignment language—not firmness marketing copy.
A mattress that scores high on Overall but weak on Pressure may still fail for dedicated side sleepers. Start with Pressure ≥ 8.0, then validate Support ≥ 7.5 for your body weight band.

sections.heading: How to use the Sleep Quiz without bias
paragraphs: The /quiz flow tags lateral (side) posture and routes you to /best-picks with weighted ranking. Treat the Top 3 matrix as a shortlist, then open each registry dossier for pros/cons tied to your budget tier.
We do not run warehouse roll tests; scores fuse retailer specs with multi-source review NLP. That makes the tool strong for narrowing SKUs, not for predicting day-one feel.

sections.heading: Checklist before clicking out to a merchant
paragraphs: Confirm trial/warranty on the brand’s PDP—our audit variant (e.g., Queen) should match the size you configure at checkout.
Open /deals if you need coupon language; copied codes fire analytics but do not change our score formulas.
Read /disclosure for affiliate routing: approved brands exit via /go/[slug] with CJ or Impact wrappers.

title: Wool Comforter vs Down Alternative: Evidence-Based Comparison
description: Merino wool vs synthetic down-alternative comforters—thermal indices, durability signals, and when each fill wins in owner-review data.

sections.heading: Thermal behavior in our Cooling index
paragraphs: Wool comforters (e.g., Sleep & Beyond MyMerino) often lead Cooling when reviews praise year-round breathability. Down-alternative fills (e.g., FluffCo) may score slightly lower on raw cooling but higher on Pressure when loft softness dominates feedback.
Use the head-to-head compare page for the same axes on both SKUs rather than relying on star averages from a single retailer.

sections.heading: Durability and care narratives
paragraphs: Durability in our model tracks sagging, clumping, and fill migration language—not years-of-warranty alone. Wool audits frequently cite lanolin care and occasional dry-clean recommendations; synthetics emphasize machine wash convenience.
If you own pets or wash frequently, weigh Durability alongside listed care constraints in each product’s specs matrix.

sections.heading: Price and affiliate transparency
paragraphs: Shelf prices in the registry reflect latest scanner passes; stack discounts may appear in /deals. Both brands in this guide participate in affiliate programs disclosed on /disclosure—commissions do not input into score math.

title: How to Read a SleepChoice Audit Page
description: Decode Overall, Support, Cooling, Pressure, and Durability indices, Cross-Platform Signals, and when to trust aggregated vs lab-style claims.

sections.heading: The five-axis scorecard
paragraphs: Each /registry/[slug] dossier publishes five indices (0–10 scale) derived from forensic_engine NLP on reviews and listing text. Overall is a weighted composite—not an average of star ratings.
Radar charts visualize the same data; large gaps between Cooling and Support usually indicate a specialized SKU (e.g., hot-sleep latex vs dense hybrid).

sections.heading: Cross-Platform Signals vs audit note
paragraphs: Cross-Platform Signals summarize brand_social_corpus snippets (Reddit-first) into sentiment and issue tags. They add community color but do not override the main audit note written during batch sync.
Visit /intelligence for a feed of recent platform batches across the catalog.

sections.heading: Journal entries and compare matrices
paragraphs: Product dossiers live at /registry/[slug] (legacy /journal URLs redirect there).
For purchase decisions between two SKUs, prefer /compare/[pair] pages with editorial intros and identical spec rows.

title: Saatva Mattress Lineup Overview: Classic, HD, Zenhaven, Rx
description: Map Saatva’s hybrid and latex models to SleepChoice registry slugs, compare shortcuts, and which audit scores differentiate each tier.

sections.heading: Classic — default hybrid shortlist
paragraphs: Saatva Classic is the canonical entry in our reddit_audit_slugs mapping and calculator default when room fit passes. It balances Support and Pressure for mixed sleepers.
Compare against HD if you are over ~230 lb or want higher support index language in owner reviews.

sections.heading: HD and Rx — capacity and positioning
paragraphs: HD emphasizes reinforced support for heavier bodies; Rx layers clinical/marketing framing. Use saatva-hd-vs-saatva-rx compare for side-by-side indices.
Configure AFFILIATE_CJ_SAATVA in production so /go routes earn commission—see .env.local.example.

sections.heading: Zenhaven — latex alternative path
paragraphs: Zenhaven trades coil motion for latex uniformity; Cooling often leads vs Classic in hot-sleeper threads. Compare via saatva-classic-vs-saatva-zenhaven before buying.
White-glove delivery and return policies differ by model—verify on Saatva PDP after leaving our /go gateway.
